title: Release Notes Style Guide
body: Release notes should start with a brief summary of the release, followed by a bullet list of user-facing features and fixes. Use the heading 'New Features' for enhancements and 'Bug Fixes' for resolved issues. Keep each entry concise—one sentence per item. Avoid internal implementation details, code references, or confidential information. Use active voice and address the end user directly. For example: 'Dark mode support added for reduced eye strain.' Do not list tasks, IDs, or engineering jargon. End with a 'Known Issues' section if applicable, but only include issues that are already public.

title: Release Notes - v3.2 Draft
body: Draft placeholder for Release Notes v3.2. This article will be updated with the final list of user-facing features and fixes once the release is finalized.
